stories.title: Bud Cauley Wins RBC Canadian Open — First Career Win at 36 After Car Accident Comeback
stories.angle: Bud Cauley, 36, won his first PGA Tour title at the 2026 RBC Canadian Open at TPC Toronto at Osprey Valley's North Course in his 239th Tour start — winning in rain and wind on Sunday. He shot 69-63-66-65 for 17-under 263, beating Matt Fitzpatrick by two shots. Cauley was a passenger in a car crash that gave him six broken ribs, a collapsed right lung, and a fractured left leg. He returned to full Tour status and made the most of every start since. Earned $1.764 million and a spot in The Open Championship at Royal Birkdale.

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
BUD CAULEY WINS THE RBC CANADIAN OPEN. 🏆

First PGA Tour title. 239th start. 36 years old.

He came back from a car crash that gave him broken ribs, a collapsed lung, and a fractured leg.

Today, on a rainy Sunday in Toronto, he finally got it. 🙌

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Bud Cauley's winning number at the RBC Canadian Open: 17-under 263. Two clear of Fitzpatrick.

He earned $1.76 million, a spot in The Open at Royal Birkdale, and something no prize money can buy — his first win.

239 starts. Worth every one.

article.headline: Bud Cauley Wins RBC Canadian Open: First PGA Tour Title at 36 in His 239th Start

research.facts:
Bud Cauley Wins RBC Canadian Open
**Verified Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- Cauley won 2026 RBC Canadian Open at TPC Toronto at Osprey Valley's North Course
- Score: 69-63-66-65 = 17-under 263
- Runner-up: Matt Fitzpatrick, two shots back
- Third: Viktor Hovland (one shot behind Fitzpatrick)
- 239th PGA Tour start (multiple sources confirm)
- Age: 36
- First PGA Tour title (multiple sources: ESPN, Golf Channel, Golf Canada, Yahoo Sports)
- Won in "rain and wind" on Sunday (PGA Tour daily wrapup confirms)
- Car accident details: passenger in car crash, sustained six broken ribs, collapsed right lung, fractured left leg (ESPN, AthlonSports)
- Prize: $1.764 million
- Also qualified for The Open Championship at Royal Birkdale via Canadian Open being part of Open Qualifying Series
- Started final round behind Jackson Suber
- Birdie run on back nine: hole-out from 93 feet on 12, made birdies on 11 (4 ft), 13 (13.5 ft), 15 (15 ft)
research.sources: - Sources: ESPN, Golf Channel, Golf Canada, Golf Monthly, Yahoo Sports, PGA Tour, AthlonSports

stories.title: US Open Preview — Scheffler's Grand Slam Bid at Shinnecock Hills, Starts Thursday
stories.angle: The 2026 US Open begins Thursday, June 18, at Shinnecock Hills Golf Club in Southampton, New York — a par 70, 7,400-yard test that has hosted the championship five previous times. Scottie Scheffler is the No. 1 favorite at +550 and is chasing the career Grand Slam — he already has two Masters titles, the 2025 PGA Championship, and the 2025 British Open. A win Sunday would complete it — on his 30th birthday (June 21), which is also Father's Day. Rain forecast for Thursday (65%), winds 15-20 mph. Rory McIlroy second favorite at +1200.

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
The US Open starts THURSDAY at Shinnecock Hills.

Scottie Scheffler (+550) is chasing the career Grand Slam.

He already has: Masters x2, 2025 PGA, 2025 British Open.

He needs: THIS.

The final round is June 21 — his 30th birthday — which is also Father's Day.

What a setup. ⛳

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Shinnecock Hills. Par 70. 7,400 yards. Rain Thursday. Wind all week.

If Scheffler wins Sunday, he becomes the 7th player in history to complete the modern career Grand Slam.

He's the clear No. 1 favorite at +550. Rory McIlroy next at +1200.

3 days away. ⛳ #USOpen

article.headline: 2026 US Open Preview: Scheffler's Grand Slam Bid Begins Thursday at Shinnecock Hills

stories.title: LIV Golf Financial Crisis — Saudi PIF Has Funded Only $200M of $600M, O'Neil Seeking Investors
stories.angle: LIV Golf's ability to complete the 2026 season remains uncertain. Financial Times reported Saudi Arabia's PIF has provided approximately $200 million of the roughly $600 million committed — funding only about one-third of what is needed. PIF has ended its long-term financial backing after 2026, citing that "substantial investment required by LIV Golf over a longer term is no longer consistent with the current phase of PIF's investment strategy." CEO Scott O'Neil has had 5 formal investor meetings with 18 more planned, calling the response "positive." Final four events remain "on the fence" per a high-ranking executive.

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
LIV Golf update: Saudi PIF has funded roughly $200M of the $600M committed for 2026.

PIF says it's ENDING financial backing after this season.

CEO Scott O'Neil says he's had 5 investor meetings with 18 more planned.

Every remaining tournament is "on the fence."

article.headline: LIV Golf Financial Crisis: Saudi PIF Has Funded Just $200M of $600M, Final Events in Doubt

research.facts:
LIV Golf Financial Crisis
**Verified Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- PIF funded approximately $200M total (FT report via Golf Digest says "only around $200M" vs $600M committed)
- Earlier: $66M in May + $130M in June = ~$196M (from prior pipeline notes, aligns with $200M figure)
- PIF has announced end to financial backing after 2026 season
- PIF statement: "substantial investment required by LIV Golf over a longer term is no longer consistent with the current phase of PIF's investment strategy"
- CEO Scott O'Neil: 5 formal investor meetings with 18 more planned, response "positive"
- High-ranking executive from league partner: "every remaining tournament is on the fence"
- Louisiana event was postponed (from prior pipeline notes)
- Final four events remain uncertain
research.sources: - Sources: Financial Times (via Golf Digest), CBS Sports, Yahoo Sports, SGI Europe, Tennis World USA, Seoul Economic Daily

stories.title: LPGA This Week — Meijer Classic Starts Thursday; KPMG Women's PGA in 10 Days
stories.angle: The LPGA Tour heads to Blythefield Country Club in Belmont, Michigan this week for the Meijer LPGA Classic for Simply Give (June 18-21). Nelly Korda enters with momentum — she won the 2026 US Women's Open at Riviera Country Club and is the two-time defending Meijer Classic champion. The bigger prize: the KPMG Women's PGA Championship on June 25-28 at Hazeltine National Golf Club in Chaska, Minnesota — a second major opportunity in three weeks. The women's major season is at its peak.

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Women's golf is entering its biggest stretch of the year:

📍 Meijer LPGA Classic — June 18-21, Blythefield CC, Michigan
🏆 KPMG Women's PGA Championship — June 25-28, Hazeltine

Nelly Korda arrives with a US Women's Open win in hand.

Two big weeks ahead. ⛳ #LPGA

article.headline: LPGA's Biggest Two Weeks: Meijer Classic Starts Thursday, KPMG Women's PGA in 10 Days

stories.title: Scheffler's Grand Slam Birthday: What Winning the US Open on June 21 Would Mean
stories.angle: Scottie Scheffler turns 30 on Sunday, June 21 — the final round of the US Open. That Sunday is also Father's Day. Scheffler is already the world's No. 1 player, a two-time Masters champion, and the holder of the 2025 PGA and 2025 Open Championship. A US Open title at Shinnecock Hills would make him the seventh player in history to complete the modern career Grand Slam (joining Gene Sarazen, Ben Hogan, Gary Player, Jack Nicklaus, Tiger Woods, Rory McIlroy). The storylines — birthday, Father's Day, Grand Slam — converge in one of the most dramatic setups in recent major history.

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
On Sunday, June 21:

Scheffler could complete the career Grand Slam.

It's also his 30th birthday.

It's also Father's Day.

4 majors in hand. Needs ONE more. Stars are aligned.

Sunday can't come soon enough. ⛳ #USOpen

article.headline: Scheffler's Grand Slam, 30th Birthday, and Father's Day: Golf's Most Perfect Story Setup

research.facts:
Scheffler Birthday/Grand Slam Feature
**Verified Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- Scheffler born June 21, 1996 → turns 30 on June 21, 2026
- June 21, 2026 = Father's Day (third Sunday of June)
- Grand Slam members (modern era, career): Gene Sarazen, Ben Hogan, Gary Player, Jack Nicklaus, Tiger Woods — McIlroy completed it
- Wait: McIlroy — did he complete the career Grand Slam? He's won Masters (yes, 2024 Augusta), US Open (2011), Open (2014), PGA (2012). So yes, McIlroy completed it. So there are 6 current holders, Scheffler would be 7th.
- Scheffler's majors: Masters x2, 2025 PGA Championship, 2025 British Open — missing only US Open
research.sources: - Sources: SI.com, CBS Sports, lineups.com, madknows.com
